name: Chocolate Cream Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Grease and flour a 10-inch springform pan.
3. Melt chocolate chips and butter in the top of a double boiler over hot, but not boiling, water.
4. Beat yolks in a large bowl with an electric mixer until very thick, about 5 minutes.
5. Beat in half cup sugar one tablespoon at a time.
6. Stir in melted chocolate, pecans, one tablespoon liqueur, and vanilla.
7. Beat whites with cream of tartar in another large bowl until soft peaks form.
8. Gradually add remaining half cup sugar and beat until stiff but not dry.
9. Gently fold one fourth of whites into chocolate mixture, then fold chocolate mixture back into remaining whites.
10. Pour batter into prepared pan.
11. Bake at 350°F for 30 minutes. Reduce oven temperature to 275°F and continue baking for another 30 minutes.
12. Turn off the oven; let cake stand in oven with door ajar for 30 minutes.
13. Remove cake from oven. Dampen paper towel and place on top of cake for 5 minutes; remove towel (top of cake will crack and fall).
14. Cool cake in pan. Remove springform. Transfer cake to platter.
15. Beat whipping cream in a large bowl with an electric mixer until soft peaks form.
16. Beat in powdered sugar and liqueur.
17. Spoon whipped cream onto top of cake and smooth evenly.
18. Sprinkle with chocolate curls if desired.
19. Refrigerate for 6 hours. Let stand at room temperature for 30 minutes before serving.
